gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (171/ 9)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (171/ 9)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَا أَبَا عَمْرٍو أَ رَأَيْتَكَ لَوْ حَدَّثْتُكَ بِحَدِيثٍ أَوْ أَفْتَيْتُكَ بِفُتْيَا ثُمَّ جِئْتَنِي بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ فَسَأَلْتَنِي عَنْهُ فَأَخْبَرْتُكَ بِخِلَافِ مَا كُنْتُ أَخْبَرْتُكَ أَوْ أَفْتَيْتُكَ بِخِلَافِ ذَلِكَ بِأَيِّهِمَا كُنْتَ تَأْخُذُ قُلْتُ بِأَحْدَثِهِمَا وَ أَدَعُ الْآخَرَ

فَقَالَ قَدْ أَصَبْتَ يَا أَبَا عَمْرٍو أَبَى اللَّهُ إِلَّا أَنْ يُعْبَدَ سِرّاً أَمَا وَ اللَّهِ لَئِنْ فَعَلْتُمْ ذَلِكَ إِنَّهُ لَخَيْرٌ لِي وَ لَكُمْ وَ أَبَى اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَنَا وَ لَكُمْ فِي دِينِهِ إِلَّا التَّقِيَّةَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Al Hassan Bin Mahboub, from Hisham Bin Salim, from Abu Amro Al Kinany who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘O Abu Amro! What is your view if I<sup>asws</sup> were to narrate to you a Hadeeth or issue a Verdict to you, then you come over to me after that and ask me about it, so I<sup>asws</sup> inform you with different to what I<sup>asws</sup> had informed you beforehand, or issue a verdict different (statement) to that, which of the two would you be taking with?’ I said, ‘With the latest of the two and leave the other one’.

So he<sup>asws</sup> said:’ You are correct, O Abu Amro! Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Refused except that He<sup>azwj</sup> be worshipped in secret. But, by Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, if you all were to do that it would be better for me<sup>asws</sup> and for you all; and Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic Refused for us<sup>asws</sup> and you all with regards to His<sup>azwj</sup> Religion, except for the dissimulation’.
en.sarwar: 7. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from al-Hassan ibn Mahbub from Hisham ibn Salim from abu ‘Amr al-Kinani who has said the following: “Once abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) said, ‘O abu ‘Amr, what would you do, were I to narrate a Hadith to you or give you a Fatwa (a legal opinion), and you came to me sometimes later to ask about the same issues, but I gave you a Hadith or a Fatwa that contradicts the first one, which one would you follow?’ I said, ‘I will follow the latest one and ignore the other one.’ The Imam said, ‘O abu ‘Amr, you are right. Allah refuses to accept anything but to worship Him secretly. By Allah, were you to follow al-Taqiyyah it would be better for you and for me. Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, refuses to accept anything from you and I in His religion but al-Taqiyyah.’”
